pattern: Only the x-coordinates matter, since a vertical strip has infinite height -- the widest empty strip is simply the largest gap between consecutive x-values once they are sorted.
hints: The y-coordinates are irrelevant here: a vertical area spans all heights, so a point only blocks a strip through its x-coordinate.
Sort just the x-coordinates, then look at the gap between each pair of neighbors in that sorted order.
The answer is the single largest of those neighbor gaps -- no point can ever lie strictly between two x-values that are already adjacent once sorted.

edge_inputs.description: four points, tightest gap is one
six points with a clear widest gap
exactly two points at the minimum allowed count
several points sharing the same x, only distinct x-values create gaps
points already given in sorted x order
points given in reverse x order

stress.description: random points over the full coordinate range in random order
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 2000))
    points = [[rng.randint(0, 1000000000), rng.randint(0, 1000000000)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [points]
